data.description:

Black Sesame International Holding Limited ( (HK:2533) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Black Sesame International Holding Limited, a company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates under a board structure that includes executive, non-executive, and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical governance framework for a publicly traded firm.

The company has announced the current composition of its board of directors and the allocation of roles across its three key board committees: Audit, Remuneration, and Nomination. Chairman and CEO Mr. Shan Jizhang and Chief System Officer Mr. Zeng Daibing serve as executive directors, supported by non-executive and independent non-executive directors who chair and staff the committees, with independent directors leading the Audit and Remuneration Committees and the chairman leading the Nomination Committee. This structure underscores the company’s emphasis on corporate governance, oversight, and separation of duties as it fulfills its obligations as a Hong Kong–listed entity.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2533) stock is a Sell with a HK$20.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Black Sesame International Holding Limited stock, see the HK:2533 Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Black Sesame Co-founder Liu Weihong Steps Down as President, Moves to Non-executive Role
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Black Sesame International Holding Limited ( (HK:2533) ) has shared an update.

Black Sesame International Holding Limited has announced that co-founder and current executive director Liu Weihong has been re-designated as a non-executive director and has stepped down as president of the company, effective 29 January 2026, citing insufficient time for day-to-day management due to personal commitments. Liu, who will remain closely involved as a consultant advising on business operations and retains significant shareholdings and share options in the group, will not receive director’s fees in his new role; the move marks a shift in the company’s senior management structure while preserving continuity in strategic guidance at board level for shareholders and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2533) stock is a Sell with a HK$20.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Black Sesame International Holding Limited stock, see the HK:2533 Stock Forecast page.

More about Black Sesame International Holding Limited

Black Sesame International Holding Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates through a group structure and is co-founded by automotive industry veteran Liu Weihong and chairman and CEO Shan Jizhang. The Group focuses on automotive-related technologies and solutions, leveraging its founders’ deep experience in strategy, operations, business development, and mergers and acquisitions across global automotive markets.

Average Trading Volume: 9,563,167

An update from Yeebo (International Holdings) Limited ( (HK:0259) ) is now available.

Yeebo (International Holdings) Limited has warned that its associate Suzhou QingYue Optoelectronics Technology expects to post a larger loss for 2025, with estimated net loss attributable to its owners ranging from RMB74 million to RMB110 million, compared with a loss of about RMB69 million in 2024, based on unaudited preliminary figures. The widening loss at Suzhou QingYue, a key OLED and e-paper display asset in which Yeebo holds about 28.08%, could weigh on Yeebo’s share of associate results and underscores ongoing profitability pressures in the advanced display segment, prompting the company to caution shareholders and potential investors when dealing in its shares.

More about Yeebo (International Holdings) Limited

Yeebo (International Holdings) Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates through its subsidiaries and holds an approximately 28.08% stake in Suzhou QingYue Optoelectronics Technology, a Shanghai-listed associate engaged in developing, manufacturing and selling OLED displays, e-paper modules and micro-OLED products for the optoelectronics and display markets in mainland China.

CF PharmTech. Inc. Class H ( (HK:2652) ) has shared an update.

CF PharmTech, Inc. has had its marketing authorization application for a budesonide nasal spray accepted by China’s National Medical Products Administration, covering indications such as seasonal and perennial allergic and non-allergic rhinitis as well as prevention and treatment of nasal polyps in patients aged six and above. The move expands the company’s already diversified nasal spray portfolio and underscores its strategic push in the respiratory inhalation segment, though the company cautions that acceptance is only an initial administrative step and that regulatory approval is not guaranteed given the inherent risks and uncertainties in drug development and commercialization.

data.title: Jiangsu Lopal Tech Forecasts Sharply Narrowed Loss for 2025 Amid Margin Pressure
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jiangsu Lopal Tech Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2465) ) has shared an announcement.

Jiangsu Lopal Tech. Group Co., Ltd. has issued an inside information announcement estimating that it will remain loss-making in 2025, but with a markedly narrower loss compared with 2024. The company projects net loss attributable to shareholders of between RMB180.17 million and RMB147.99 million, a substantial improvement from last year’s loss of about RMB645 million, and expects net loss after deducting non-recurring items to shrink to roughly RMB29.19–39.50 million from nearly RMB698.47 million previously. Management attributes the continued losses to low gross margins in its lithium iron phosphate cathode materials business amid lithium carbonate price volatility, as well as inventory and asset impairments and significant non-recurring losses related to fair value changes of financial liabilities. Nonetheless, the company notes that cost-cutting, product differentiation, overseas expansion efforts, and efficiency initiatives have significantly reduced its loss, suggesting gradual operational stabilization despite ongoing margin and market pressures.

More about Jiangsu Lopal Tech Co. Ltd. Class H

Jiangsu Lopal Tech. Group Co., Ltd. is a China-based company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the lithium materials sector, with a key focus on lithium iron phosphate cathode materials used in batteries. Its business is sensitive to raw material price fluctuations, particularly lithium carbonate, and its profitability is also influenced by inventory valuation and asset impairment trends.

Average Trading Volume: 14,491,749

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$11.88B

data.title: Dingyi Group Buys Majority Stake in Zambian Gold Mining License Holder
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Dingyi Group Investment Limited ( (HK:0508) ).

Dingyi Group Investment Limited has agreed to acquire a 70% equity interest in a Zambian holding company that controls a mining license granting exclusive rights to develop and mine gold and other mineral deposits at a site near Lusaka, Zambia. The US$3.42 million initial cash consideration for the stake, which may be adjusted based on verified gold deposit quantities and includes profit‑sharing rights for the vendors, will be funded through a shareholder loan from Dingyi to its purchasing subsidiary. Concurrently, Dingyi is entering into a shareholders’ agreement with a manager‑owned entity, under which the manager will provide mine administration services and hold a minority interest in the purchaser, leaving the Zambian target as an indirect, non‑wholly owned subsidiary with Dingyi holding effective control. As the mining asset is at a greenfield stage with no prior exploration or operations, the transaction marks a strategic entry into early‑stage gold mining in Africa and is classified as a disclosable transaction under Hong Kong Listing Rules, signaling a potentially higher‑risk, higher‑reward diversification of Dingyi’s portfolio that could reshape its resource‑sector positioning if the mine proves commercially viable.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0508) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.62 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dingyi Group Investment Limited stock, see the HK:0508 Stock Forecast page.

More about Dingyi Group Investment Limited

Dingyi Group Investment Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong (Stock Code: 508), operates through investment holdings and is expanding into natural resources by acquiring interests in mining assets overseas. The company is now targeting gold and other mineral deposits in Zambia, reflecting a strategic move into African mining projects via indirect, non-wholly owned subsidiaries and partnerships with local operators and managers.

Average Trading Volume: 611,282

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$595.5M

China Sunshine Paper Holdings Co. Ltd. ( (HK:2002) ) has issued an announcement.

China Sunshine Paper Holdings has announced boardroom changes effective 29 January 2026, with non-executive director Zhang Xiaohui and independent non-executive director Wang Zefeng resigning due to personal engagements and relinquishing their roles on the remuneration, nomination and audit committees. Both departing directors stated they had no claims against the company or disagreements with the board, while the company simultaneously appointed industry veteran Chen Dongxu as a non-executive director and Li Hengyuan as an independent non-executive director, moves that refresh the board’s composition and committee leadership and may influence the group’s governance and strategic oversight without disrupting continuity. Mr. Chen brings over three decades of management experience, including senior roles at Shanghai-listed Xiamen C&D Inc., and will serve a three-year term without director’s service fees, underscoring a focus on experienced oversight and alignment with Hong Kong listing requirements.

C&D Newin Paper & Pulp Corporation Limited has announced an updated composition of its board of directors effective 29 January 2026, comprising two executive directors, including Chief Executive Officer Mr. Huang Tiansheng, two non-executive directors, and two independent non-executive directors, reflecting a balanced governance structure for a Hong Kong-listed paper and pulp company. data.title: C&D Newin Paper & Pulp Announces Director’s Passing and Boardroom Reshuffle
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

C&D Newin Paper & Pulp Corporation Limited ( (HK:0731) ) has provided an update.

C&D Newin Paper & Pulp Corporation Limited announced the passing of independent non-executive director and audit committee chairman Ernest Wong Yiu Kit, leaving the board and audit committee temporarily non-compliant with Hong Kong Listing Rules regarding the minimum number of independent directors and audit committee members. The company said it will use its best endeavours to appoint a new independent non-executive director and audit committee chair within the prescribed three-month period, and will update the market when a suitable candidate is identified. Separately, chairman and non-executive director Zhang Xiaohui has resigned from the board and its remuneration and nomination committees, as well as from his role as an authorised representative, citing personal engagements and confirming no disagreement with the board. The board has appointed Chen Dongxu as the new chairman, non-executive director, committee member and authorised representative with effect from 29 January 2026, restoring compliance with the requirement to have two authorised representatives and signalling a swift leadership transition amid the broader boardroom reshuffle.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0731) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.19 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on C&D Newin Paper & Pulp Corporation Limited stock, see the HK:0731 Stock Forecast page.

More about C&D Newin Paper & Pulp Corporation Limited

C&D Newin Paper & Pulp Corporation Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in Bermuda, operating in the paper and pulp sector. The company is subject to Hong Kong Listing Rules and maintains a typical governance structure that includes independent non-executive directors, an audit committee, a remuneration committee, and a nomination committee to oversee its corporate governance and regulatory compliance obligations.
